chemistry.scope_summary: Thiol-ene reaction between N-Boc L-cysteine methyl ester and 4-penten-1-ol under visible light irradiation

figures.key_information: Photochemical thiol-ene reaction with residence time of 2 minutes, using Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2 catalyst (1 mol%), p-toluidine (0.5 equiv), in MeCN solvent with blue LED illumination

figures.caption: Scheme 1. Photochemical Thiol − Ene Reaction for the Alkylation of a Protected Cysteine Derivative | Figure 1.  Continuous  flow setup for the photochemical thiol − ene reaction for the alkylation of a protected cysteine derivative. | Figure 2.  Batch setup for the photochemical thiol − ene reaction for the alkylation of a protected cysteine derivative.

figures.flow_path: continuous flow through reactor with blue LED irradiation
figures.key_information: Photocatalytic thiol-ene coupling reaction using Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2 catalyst (1 mol%), p-toluidine (0.5 equiv), blue LED irradiation, MeCN solvent, residence time = 2 min
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Photochemical Thiol − Ene Reaction for the Alkylation of a Protected Cysteine Derivative | Figure 1.  Continuous  flow setup for the photochemical thiol − ene reaction for the alkylation of a protected cysteine derivative. | Figure 2.  Batch setup for the photochemical thiol − ene reaction for the alkylation of a protected cysteine derivative.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Traditional batch setup using glass dish with blue LED illumination
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous flow setup allows for precise control of residence time and improved light exposure
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design from glass dish to PFA coil reactor with LED wrapped around
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photocatalyst (Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2), additive (p-toluidine), solvent (acetonitrile), and blue LED light source
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow reduces handling of reaction mixture

figure_descriptions: Photoreactor scheme showing cylindrical reactor with external blue LED
Flow reactor setup with blue LED irradiation

notes: This is a teaching laboratory experiment paper. Quantitative data (yields, exact reaction times, concentrations) are not provided as the focus is on educational methodology rather than optimization. Photocatalyst loading and additive equivalents were derived from figure information.
